changelog.summary: v3.7 Phase C: new World → Countries lens + data/countries.json, 12 curated nations (US, China, Taiwan, South Korea, Japan, India, Singapore, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Netherlands, Germany, Russia). Schema per country: dynamics{demography,energy,chips,fiscal,stability,trajectory}, gov_focus[{vector,intensity_0_100,direction,basis}], flows[{to_iso,type,magnitude_0_100,direction,basis}], links[] to real existing atlas/tree/constraint ids, confidence:"asserted" throughout since every score is an editorial synthesis, not a measured statistic — every field carries a basis string saying why. Demography numbers reuse the existing constraints.json UN-WPP-sourced figures rather than re-deriving. Three lenses: Dynamics (small-multiples grid, sortable by any variable), Gov Focus (heatmap, countries × policy vectors derived dynamically from the data — no hardcoded vector list), Capital Flows (lightweight equirectangular SVG — deliberately not the atlas canvas's own pan/zoom projection, which is tied to that canvas's specific transform state; borrows its visual language — dashed animated arcs, motionOK-gated — not its rendering machinery). Extended the existing renderCountryDossier (previously demography-only, DEMO-map-driven, covering ~147 ISO codes) to layer in the rich dossier for the curated 12 while leaving the other ~135 on the original view. fromStoreId/xchipFor/search (Cmd-K) all learned the country. prefix. js/validate.js gained a full countries schema check (score ranges, basis-required, link resolution). Real bug fixed during build: named the new country-list variable COUNTRIES, which silently clobbered an existing COUNTRIES var already used by the Atlas map's own demography-choropleth rendering (same scope, same name, different meaning) — renamed to CTRY_LIST.

v3.6 EVERYTHING Phases B-D: new Everything sub-view on the Trends toolbar. SVG timeline 1990-2035, 6 curated house trends, two lines per trend (belief/reality) with a shaded gap area (widest labeled EDGE), solid-observed/dashed-projected with a widening uncertainty cone, milestone dots from dated tree events, an X marker at each curve's falsifier/close year. Hover dims other curves (reused Tree tab's applyTreeLighting technique); click opens the underlying dossier (added a kind-aware dispatch since theses don't use the generic dossier panel). Controls: Only Rising/Declining filters, Indexed (rescale each curve to its own peak=100), Small Multiples (grid of sparklines - the mobile default, and the actual fix for the spaghetti-chart complaint), Fullscreen, year-scrub reusing the Tree-rewind/Trajectory-scrubber slider pattern. Accountability: each legend chip tagged DERIVED or AUTHORED (render code now actually checks for an authored data/*.json curve[] before falling back to derivation, not just documented in schema); a DUE-flagged 'Log as forecast' button pre-fills the existing Forecasts tab form with real dated/sourced text and hands off to the existing Brier/calibration pipeline rather than inventing a parallel one. Categorical palette (6 hues reused from the Atlas layer legend) validated with the dataviz skill's script, all 4 checks pass. Four real bugs fixed during preview testing: a stale-DOM-id lookup that silently broke hover-dim, a dossier-open assumption that didn't hold for theses, a label de-collision pass that didn't reserve space for the two-line EDGE badge, and a NOW-label heuristic borrowed from Trajectory that mislabeled past years when scrubbed below the current year.

v3.5 Phase A: curve derivation engine for EVERYTHING (the accountable multi-trend intensity chart). Optional curve[{year,belief_0_100,reality_0_100,band_0_100?}] field documented on windows.json/theses.json — none authored yet, so every curve is computed by new js/engine.js evtg* functions from real existing fields: theses derive belief from crowd_awareness/awareness_trend, reality from trajectory.points normalized by distance-from-kill_threshold (polarity-safe — doesn't require knowing per-thesis whether a higher raw metric is good or bad) where a trajectory exists, else reality = belief + consensus_delta_num; windows derive reality from status/opened/expected_close (ramp-plateau, decay after close), belief as reality lagged ~2yr scaled 0.85, converging to reality once a window is closed (hindsight). Every derived curve is tagged derived:true, distinct from an eventual authored curve. js/validate.js gained a curve[] schema check (monotonic years, 0-100 ranges) for whenever one is authored.

v3.4 Phase D: 6 new models.json entries (Value Created vs Value Captured, 7 Powers, Value Migration, Smiling Curve, Picks and Shovels, Aggregation Theory), each wired into a real Ledger UI overlay per the no-Models-tab rule. New HELM section 7, Capture Blindspot: cross-references pilot.active_bets against every ledger.json layer via word-overlap matching (2+ shared significant words, with a stoplist for generic AI-domain words and light suffix-stripping so 'switching cost' matches a layer's moat.type 'switching costs') to flag bets sitting in LEAK layers, and separately surfaces layers marked solo_accessible (new required ledger.json field per layer) with a moat type a solo/small team could plausibly hold. Every rec cites the layer id and moat.decay_note as its kill condition. js/validate.js extended to require solo_accessible on every layer.

v3.3 Phase C: new Ledger tab (works on mobile). Domain picker + house thesis + a migration scrubber (2020-2030, linear interpolation between authored years) reusing the tree-rewind/constraints-clock slider pattern. Each layer renders as a diverging value_created/value_captured bar around a center spine carrying the moat-type chip, colored by gap (rent=red, leak=blue, moat=white, aligned within +/-8). Money-flow SVG overlay: particles animate top-to-bottom, thinning past higher-capture layers, gated by prefers-reduced-motion same as the starfield/atlas-arcs. Layer dossier: mechanism, capture metric with a distinct ASSERTED badge (not a misleading LOW) when no metric is disclosed, moat strength bar, migration trend, and grouped cross-links (atlas/tree as clickable chips, constraints as inert badges). Extended the INVERT button to handle ledger dossiers (previously only thesis/trend/human — a matching gap for atlas/tree dossiers was noticed but left alone, out of scope). Fixed a second hardcoded tab list inside the Router dispatch handler that had drifted out of sync with TAB_ORDER and was silently missing every tab shipped after the original v2 build.

v3.2 Phase B: new data/ledger.json — value created vs. value captured across a value chain, per domain→layers. Schema: value_created{score_0_100,mechanism,basis}, value_captured{score_0_100,metric,basis,confidence}, moat{type (7 Powers taxonomy or 'none'),strength_0_100,decay_note}, migration[{year,pool_share_0_100}], links[] to existing atlas/tree/human/constraint ids. Seeded one domain fully: ai-compute (power, chips, memory-hbm, hyperscalers, foundation-models, applications, end-users) — house thesis: value creation peaks at foundation-models but capture concentrates at the physical infra floor (chips/memory/power), all cross-linked to real existing chokepoints (atlas.tsmc, atlas.asml, atlas.skhynix, atlas.pjm), tree nodes (tree.openw, tree.reasoning, tree.cloud), constraints (constraint.hbm-qualification, constraint.heavy-gas-turbine-slot), and humans (human.chang, human.huang, human.su, human.hassabis, human.sutskever, human.amodei, human.bezos, human.ellison). No precise unverified margins asserted — value_captured entries without a real disclosed metric are flagged confidence:"asserted". js/validate.js extended to check the ledger schema (score ranges, moat taxonomy, link resolution).

v3.1 Phase A: rebrand ORRERY → MAGNITUDE. Wordmark "MAGNITUDE" + monogram "OM", subtitle "Orders of Magnitude · Slow Variables", tagline "finds the order-of-magnitude gaps — value vs. capture, consensus vs. reality, price vs. truth." Masthead, title, OG/Twitter meta + regenerated OG card, new favicon.svg, README, package.json updated. No data or tab-logic changes.

v3.0: rebrand to ORRERY ("a runnable instrument, not a static reference") across four phases. (A) NASA design layer — rust accent, procedural starfield, engineering title block, mission-status glyphs (◉✕⊙◈) on thesis/window cards, no data changes. (B) Orbital Atlas (radial schematic toggle on the map — time-to-lock as radius, domain as angle) + new Trajectory tab (NOW→2050 per-thesis charts with uncertainty cones and kill_watch threshold lines; added the optional theses.json `trajectory` field to 3/5 theses, honestly left the other 2 uncharted). (C) New Simulate tab — causal loop diagram over a curated set of tree/atlas links now carrying sign/lag_years/sign_basis (3 real feedback loops closed: capex↔reasoning reinforcing, nvda↔export-controls balancing, pjm↔nuclear-restart balancing); Perturb mode runs deterministic discrete propagation; added model.leverage_points (Meadows' 12 Leverage Points) to models.json. (D) New Helm tab — private personal lens, renders only if a local gitignored pilot.json loads (schema documented in committed pilot.example.json), six sections each citing a data source and a falsifier.

changelog.note: Phase 0: migrated v2 data to JSON entity registry

changelog.summary: Initial v2 build: tech tree, humans, trends registries; full cross-registry xrefs; data health panel; command palette

v2.1: humans 6→19 (Chang, Son, Bezos, Thiel, Buffett, Zhang, Hassabis, Sutskever, Amodei, Su, Keller, Wang, Levels); tree 37→46 (foundry, cloud, smartphone, crypto-gpu, alphafold, reasoning, sovereign-ai, robotics-fm, stablecoin-rails); trends 10→16 (whole-earth: nuclear, defense, reshoring, sovereign-ai, GLP-1, stablecoins); features: forecast seeding from registries, trends gantt, tree rewind slider, bet anatomy scoring, hash deep-links, number-key tabs; review_after_months now mandatory on tree/humans/trends

Phase 5 (Models as Mechanics): 12-model registry with provenance tags, inversion toggle, bias auto-checker, margin-of-safety indicator, circle-of-competence strip, edge meter, second-order chains, Red Queen rent-decay bars, tipping-condition meter, scale check — every model manifests as a field/overlay/action, no Models tab

v2.2: simplification + entry-point pass across 6 phases. (1) New Today landing tab — 3 auto-computed cards, plain-English spotlight, data health + closing windows; theses gained kill_watch + plain fields; header regrouped into Understand/Act/Maintain. (2) Spine view in Thesis tab — SVG causal ribbon (thesis→constraints→atlas→tree→humans→windows) built purely from Store.neighbors()/links, THIN EVIDENCE badge for theses with <3 links. (3) Public forecast track record — data/forecasts.json (6 seeded, unresolved, honest) merged with private localStorage forecasts; public entries read-only in UI; Export button. (4) Mobile read-mode below 700px — stacked header, Atlas/Tree gated behind a Desktop Only notice, single-column Trends kanban, 38-40px tap targets. (5) README/UPDATE_PROTOCOL/CLAUDE.md + package.json + CI validator workflow. (6) Share button on thesis cards (deep-link + plain-text block) + OG image (scripts/generate-og.js, canvas) + meta tags.
